Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists PAL1 as AT2G37040.

Source_Species.reason: The paper studies Arabidopsis; text repeatedly refers to Arabidopsis and A. thaliana.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods explicitly state A. thaliana and Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species.reason: Experiments were performed in Arabidopsis seedlings/plants.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Methods specify A. thaliana as the plant material.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods state mutants/lines were in the Col-0 background and many constructs were expressed in Col-0.

Enzyme_Name.reason: PAL is phenylalanine ammonia-lyase; PAL1 is one isoform studied.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number is given anywhere in the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: PAL isoforms/family are clearly discussed, but the specific phrase 'aromatic amino acid ammonia-lyase' does not appear.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: Introduction states PAL catalyzes the first committed step in the phenylpropanoid pathway and the paper links it to flavonoid biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: PAL is explicitly upstream of flavonoids and the first committed step, so supplying the flavonoid branch is directly inferable.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Abstract/intro explicitly say PAL catalyzes the first committed step.

Primary_Substrate.reason: By enzyme identity and assay description using Phe substrate in PAL activity assay, phenylalanine is supported.

Primary_Product.reason: The paper does not explicitly state trans-cinnamic acid and ammonia in the provided text, though this is standard PAL chemistry.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: General PAL activity is supported, but the exact reaction wording is not explicitly given in the paper text.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The paper directly links PAL to phenylpropanoid flux and flavonoid production, so precursor supply for flavonoid biosynthesis is supported.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results measure anthocyanins and flavonol glycosides as downstream products affected by nuclear PAL1.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Anthocyanins and flavonols are explicitly discussed as flavonoids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Localization was assayed in roots and metabolites were quantified from seedlings; this wording matches the experimental material.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Abstract/introduction discuss environmental adaptation and UV-B–induced flavonoid accumulation, supporting UV/stress-related function.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Results show PAL1-GFP nuclear accumulation in root meristem, enhanced in elongation zone in kfb4m, and induced by UV-B, sucrose, and NAR.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Fig. 1 and later figures show PAL1 in cytoplasm and nucleus.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: UV-B, high sucrose, NAR, and CHX recovery experiments are all explicitly described.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used for PAL1 in the Results: stable overexpression, phospho-mutants, confocal imaging, fractionation, Phos-tag, activity assay, RT-qPCR, dual-luciferase, metabolite profiling, Co-IP, and BiFC.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: PAL1 specifically interacts with TT8 by BiFC and Co-IP.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper supports PAL as the first committed step and a major control point, but does not explicitly call it rate-limiting.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show nuclear PAL1 represses flavonoid gene expression, reduces anthocyanin/flavonol glycosides, and lowers PAL activity via nuclear translocation/sequestration.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Arabidopsis materials are in the Col-0 background.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods repeatedly state constructs such as 35S:PAL1-GFP and 35S:PAL1-GFP-NLS.

Omics_Data.reason: Mass spectrometry identified phosphorylation sites and targeted UPLC/LC-MS metabolite profiling was performed; no accession is provided, consistent with the field text.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a speculative application not tested or stated in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Lignin and salicylic acid are named as PAL-derived metabolites generally, but the paper does not test tradeoffs from engineering PAL suppression.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results/discussion support phosphorylation-dependent nuclear import and state NAR is used as a representative metabolite cue rather than a proven direct signal.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are directly aligned with the PAL1 Results and Discussion.

corrections.reason: No EC number is given anywhere in the paper.

corrections.old_value: Engineering PAL localization or phosphorylation state could fine-tune flavonoid output and avoid overaccumulation; phosphosite editing or localization control may be useful but may also affect broader phenylpropanoid flux.
corrections.reason: This is a speculative application not tested or stated in the paper.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists PAL2 as AT3G53260.

Source_Species.reason: PAL2 is an Arabidopsis gene studied in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species.reason: PAL2 assays were performed in Arabidopsis transgenic lines/seedlings.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Transgenic lines were generated in Col-0 or kfb4m background; Col-0 is the base variety.

Enzyme_Name.reason: PAL2 is one of the PAL isoforms, i.e., phenylalanine ammonia-lyase 2.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number appears in the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: PAL family membership is clear, but the exact domain wording is not given.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: PAL family is placed in phenylpropanoid/flavonoid biosynthesis throughout the paper.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: As a PAL isozyme in the first committed step, PAL2 being upstream of flavonoid branch is directly inferable.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: PAL enzymes are stated to catalyze the first committed step.

Primary_Substrate.reason: PAL activity assay uses Phe and PAL identity supports phenylalanine as substrate.

Primary_Product.reason: Specific products are not explicitly stated for PAL2 in the paper text.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Exact reaction description is standard biochemistry but not explicitly written in the paper.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: PAL2 is part of the upstream PAL pool contributing to flavonoid biosynthesis per the Results.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Anthocyanins and flavonol glycosides are measured for PAL1-centered manipulations, but PAL2 itself is not directly tested for effects on these final products.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The pathway context is flavonoids, though direct PAL2 metabolite testing is limited.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The relevant metabolite assays in the paper were done on seedlings.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Flavonoids are discussed in UV-B/environmental adaptation context.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Results show PAL2 nuclear accumulation under UV and NAR in seedlings.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: PAL2 is shown in cytoplasm and nucleus in protoplasts and after induction/fractionation.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: UV-B and NAR effects on PAL2 localization are explicitly reported.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: PAL2 was tested by confocal localization, fractionation, and association with TT8 shown in supplementary experiments cited in Results.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Results state all Arabidopsis PAL isoforms including PAL2 could associate with TT8 in parallel experiments (fig. S7F).

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Family-level first committed step is supported, but 'key control point' for PAL2 specifically is not directly tested.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: PAL2 nuclear accumulation is shown, but direct demonstration that PAL2 itself suppresses flavonoid biosynthesis/final products is not provided.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods indicate PAL2-GFP constructs under 35S promoter.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: No breeding/engineering value for PAL2 is stated or tested.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: This general speculation is not evaluated for PAL2 in the paper.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion/results note PAL1 and PAL2 are primary PAL enzymes, and pal1/2 mutants have nearly no PAL expression.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Points 1 and 2 are supported, but point 3 overstates PAL2's direct contribution to feedback-controlled flavonoid biosynthesis because this was not directly tested with final-product outputs for PAL2 alone.

corrections.reason: No EC number appears in the paper.

corrections.old_value: PAL2 may be co-targeted with PAL1 to modulate phenylpropanoid entry flux via localization control.
corrections.reason: No breeding/engineering value for PAL2 is stated or tested.

corrections.old_value: Suppression may broadly affect phenylpropanoid-derived traits.
corrections.reason: This general speculation is not evaluated for PAL2 in the paper.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists TT8 as AT4G09820.

Source_Species.reason: TT8 is an Arabidopsis gene studied in this paper.

Applied_Species.reason: TT8 was tested in Arabidopsis and transiently in N. benthamiana assays, but as an Arabidopsis factor.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is the source/applied gene context for TT8 assays.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Arabidopsis assays used Col-0 background transgenic lines.

Regulator_Type.reason: Results explicitly describe TT8 as a core component of the MBW transcriptional complex; TT8 is a known transcription factor and bHLH is inferable from MBW context and common nomenclature.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results explicitly refer to TT8 as 'the bHLH factor TT8'.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: BiFC signal with TT8-PAL is nuclear, and TT8 functions in the nuclear MBW complex.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: TT8 is linked to flavonoid biosynthetic gene expression, and PAL-TT8 effects ultimately reduce anthocyanin and flavonol accumulation in the study context.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Flavonoid metabolite assays were performed on seedlings.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Flavonoids are discussed as important for UV protection/environmental adaptation.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Results explicitly state TT8 is a core MBW component regulating flavonoid biosynthetic gene transcription.

Regulation_Mode.reason: The paper shows TT8-mediated promoter activation and that PAL binding disrupts MBW complex formation and attenuates transcriptional activity.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: CHS, CHI, F3H, FLS, and DFR are the flavonoid biosynthetic genes assayed as targets in relation to PAL/MBW regulation.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Dual-luciferase assays show TT8 activation of CHI and FLS and inhibition by PAL; PAL also reduces transcript levels of CHS, CHI, F3H, FLS, and DFR in vivo.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: PAL1 and other PAL isoforms interact with TT8; TT8 also interacts with MYB75 in BiFC/MBW context.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: Results explicitly describe TT8 as part of the MBW complex with MYB75 and TTG1, and PAL disrupts MBW assembly.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: Within this paper, TT8 is presented as a core node for high flavonoid gene expression and the direct target of PAL-mediated feedback.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: BiFC, Co-IP in N. benthamiana and Arabidopsis, dual-luciferase, and interaction interference assays are all reported.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: PAL nuclear accumulation is induced by UV-B, sucrose, and NAR, and this indirectly affects TT8 activity via PAL-TT8 interaction.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: The study supports flavonoid accumulation status acting via PAL nuclear translocation to affect TT8.

Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation.reason: The paper explicitly frames this as a metabolite-triggered negative feedback loop involving PAL-TT8.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show nuclear PAL binds TT8, disrupts TT8-MYB75 interaction, reduces TT8-mediated promoter activation, and is associated with lower flavonoid accumulation.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state TT8-HA and TT8-GFP constructs were generated; 35S:TT8-GFP/Col-0 is explicitly mentioned for Arabidopsis Co-IP.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is speculative and not stated/tested in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Potential tradeoffs from hyperactivation are not examined in the paper.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results explicitly say TT8 was the only tested flavonoid MBW factor showing robust interaction with PAL, whereas TTG1 and MYB75 did not.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are directly supported by Figs. 6 and 7 and related text.

corrections.old_value: Manipulating TT8 interaction interfaces or PAL-insensitive TT8 variants may sustain flavonoid production under feedback conditions.
corrections.reason: This is speculative and not stated/tested in the paper.

corrections.old_value: Hyperactivation may cause excessive flavonoid accumulation and broader transcriptional changes.
corrections.reason: Potential tradeoffs from hyperactivation are not examined in the paper.
